channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent denies that tariff pause is due to market declines
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent denied that the president's move to implement a blanket pause on his tariffs was the result of declines in the financial markets, arguing the action was part of Trump's strategy all along.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican-led House passes bill to limit nationwide orders from federal district judges
item.description: The House has passed a bill that limits the authority of federal district judges to issue nationwide orders.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump-backed bill to stop 'rogue' judges passes House
item.description: House lawmakers advanced a bill to block district judges from issuing nationwide injunctions in most cases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump targets ‘Anonymous’ author and former top cybersecurity official in escalation of retribution
item.description: President Donald Trump is intensifying his efforts to punish his critics by signing a pair of memoranda directing the Justice Department to investigate two officials from his first administration and stripping them of any security clearances they may have.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Exclusive: Pence agrees Trump’s tariff policy a potential midterm liability for GOP
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration’s aggressive use of tariffs poses a potential risk for Republicans in the midterm elections, even as he expressed relief about a walk-back of some of the most severe tariffs on allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump lets the water flow
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: S&P 500 soars 9.5% to one of its biggest gains since WWII after Trump limits tariffs
item.description: Stocks surged to one of their biggest gains since World War II after President Donald Trump paused his tariffs against most other nations, as investors had desperately hoped he would. Trump, though, did raise tariffs further on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court allows Trump’s firings of independent agency board members to take effect, for now
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday allowed the Trump administration to oust two board members who oversee independent agencies, for now. The action seems to signal the court's support for President Donald Trump's effort to remove limits on his power to hire and fire.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington state Democrats want to tax online dating apps
item.description: Dating apps would be required to pay a love tax if a bill in Washington state gets the approval of lawmakers in an effort to fund domestic violence programs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers cut 6 new positions in Nevada Department of Public Safety budget request
item.description: Investigation backlogs and training needs within the Nevada Department of Public Safety are among the justifications for new positions in the state budget, but lawmakers said Wednesday there's just not enough money to pay for everything.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House votes to overturn Biden-era rule limiting bank overdraft fees to $5, sends to Trump to sign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The House voted Wednesday to overturn a rule that would have limited bank overdraft fees to $5, following the Senate in moving to dismantle the regulation that the Biden administration had estimated would save consumers billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: CORA LEWIS and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department sharply criticizes judge who blocked enforcement of an order targeting a law firm
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is sharply criticizing a judge who halted enforcement of a White House executive order meant to punish a prominent law firm, continuing a pattern of Trump administration attacks on the judiciary over decisions that officials don't like.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP holdouts threaten Trump’s ‘big, beautiful’ budget and tax cut bill as votes are postponed
item.description: House Republicans have abruptly postponed a late evening vote on their budget framework as grave misgivings from conservative GOP lawmakers threw it in flux.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump pauses tariffs on most nations for 90 days, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125 percent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: New poll reveals Trump's approval ratings
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: About $1.8 billion in federal money for Cornell and Northwestern is frozen, the White House says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has frozen more than $1 billion in federal funding for Cornell University and about $790 million for Northwestern University while the government investigates alleged civil rights violations at the schools, the White House said.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, CHEYANNE MUMPHREY and SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court clears the way for the Trump administration to fire thousands of probationary workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court cleared the way Wednesday for President Donald Trump's administration to fire thousands of probationary workers, halting a judge’s order requiring them to be reinstated in a legal win for Trump's effort to downsize the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senators formally introduce bill to eliminate Department of Education
item.description: Republican Senators take action to eliminate U.S. Department of Education and introduce bill to companion President Donald Trump's March executive order.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: White House rolls out implementation of AI for federal employee records
item.description: The U.S. Office of Personnel Management (OPM) will post an updated Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) at the close of business Wednesday that paves the way for a major artificial intelligence rollout.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.S. says it is now monitoring immigrants' social media for antisemitism
item.description: Effective immediately, the government says it will begin screening immigrant social media for activity that officials think indicates support for antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd, MPR News

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed off his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FEMA to revoke $188M in New York City migrant grants
item.description: The money is part of the Shelter and Services Program, which helps local governments provide for migrants.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Stocks soar after Trump announces 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for some
item.description: Although markets opened mixed on Wednesday, stocks soared after President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump should focus on ‘targeted tariffs’: Rep. James Comer
item.description: Comer also emphasized the importance of considering what must be produced in the United States from a national security perspective.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Biden believes he would've beaten Trump in election: Author
item.description: Author Chris Whipple released "Uncharted: How Trump Beat Biden, Harris, and the Odds in the Wildest Campaign in History" on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Judge orders White House to allow AP access to news events
item.description: U.S. Judge Trevor N. McFadden rules the White House cannot deny the Associated Press access to news events because the wire service continues to use “Gulf of Mexico” rather than “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Amy Coney Barrett is what is needed in Supreme Court justice: Analyst
item.description: Barrett has sided with liberal justices on multiple hot-button issues, including DOGE regulation and Arizona’s voter ID law.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Weber, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota pushes back on Trump demand to end DEI or lose school funding
item.description: Minnesota Education Commissioner Willie Jett told federal officials the state already complies with federal civil rights laws and that the Trump administration did not have the power “to unilaterally overrule the will of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Legal status revoked for 985,000 migrants who entered US under Biden-era CBP One app
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is terminating parole protections for people who entered the country through the CBP One app, revoking legal status for nearly a million migrants who came to the U.S. during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: New audit, renewed criticism of Met Council's oversight of Southwest light rail project
item.description: The release of a new audit of the Southwest Corridor light rail project in the Twin Cities prompted sharp questions and bipartisan criticism from Minnesota lawmakers on Monday. Officials with the Metropolitan Council said the line is on track to begin service in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House next week
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's Gov. Joe Lombardo's plan to solve the Nevada housing crisis
item.description: A $250 million plan to spur construction of homes and apartments for low- to middle-income Nevadans is projected to provide homes for 16,000 people, according to officials who presented it Wednesday night in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could have a profound impact on California’s exports, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
